title: quassia floribunda
intro: Quassia floribunda, also known as the Yellow Dogwood or Quassia Tree, is a tropical tree native to Central and South America, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant's roots and bark have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Quassia floribunda include treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as being used as a natural insect repellent. Quassia floribunda can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
